content: A two-tier quality system — non-negotiable invariants (always enforced) plus a selectable craft toolbox (chosen per-story during concept/plotting, then binding) — is an effective pattern for creative domains. It separates universal quality floors from story-specific technique decisions. This run had 34 invariants (I1-I34) as the always-on tier and 26 craft tools (T1-T26) as the selectable tier. The craft-profile-selector agent picks 5-8 tools per story, and adversarial auditors verify against the selected set rather than the entire toolbox. This avoids over-constraining stories while maintaining quality floors.
sourceEvidence: Part 1: 'Invariants — Non-negotiable rules that MUST hold for every story'. Part 2: 'Craft Toolbox — Proven storytelling techniques... During Concept and Plotting, the system selects which tools fit the story... Once selected for a story, a tool becomes binding.' The system selected 5-8 tools per story with auditors verifying against selected toolset only.

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
